subject: Mathematics
title: GPS and Triangulation
description: Learn GPS and Triangulation in one minute.

hook.text: Your phone knows where you are within a few meters. Geometry makes that possible.

buildup.text: GPS uses signals from at least four satellites. Each signal defines a sphere of possible locations.

discovery.text: Where three spheres intersect, you get a point. That's your position on Earth.

twist.text: The fourth satellite corrects for clock errors. Tiny timing mistakes cause huge position shifts.

climax.text: Ancient triangulation used mountains and stars. Modern GPS uses the same geometry from orbit.

punchline.text: Satellites and spheres intersect to pinpoint you on Earth.

quiz.question: How many satellites does GPS need at minimum?
